description:
fz currently has a flat global namespace. All fn/defmacro items go into one env; `Mod.fn` parses but is treated like a Var. Adds Elixir-style modules so the test runner (.16) and any non-trivial program can avoid global-name collisions.

## Scope

- Lexer: tokens already exist (Tok::Upper, Tok::Defmodule maybe new).
- Parser:
  - Top-level item `defmodule Name do <items> end` containing fn/defmacro/nested defmodule.
  - Qualified call: `Mod.fn(args)` — Expr::Call where the callee is Expr::Dot(Var("Mod"), "fn").
  - Plain calls in module body resolve to the enclosing module's fn first.
- AST:
  - Item::Module(ModuleDef { name, items }) — recursive for nested modules.
- Eval:
  - Per-module env. Lookup walks the current module then up.
  - Mod.fn dispatches by qualified name.
- Aot/Jit:
  - Mangling: ModName__fnName for compiled symbols.
  - Cross-module monomorphization: probably already works with the user_name -> mangled rename.
- Macros:
  - Macro expansion lives at the module that defined the macro; calling Mod.macro(x) expands inline.
- Imports/alias: defer if not strictly needed for tests.

## Tests

- defmodule M do; fn f(x), do: x + 1; end + main calling M.f(41) -> 42.
- Two modules each defining fn helper(x) without collision.
- Macro defined in module A, called as A.macro(x) from module B.

## Followups out of scope

- import/alias/use sugar
- Module attributes (@spec, @doc)
- Protocols / behaviours
title: Modules: defmodule + qualified calls
